channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Railing come off, 27th June
item.description:
Metal railing has come off. If this is to fall it can cause a lot of harm.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Broadway, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Zebra crossing island been hit, 26th June
item.description:
Looks like a big vehicle hit the island on the zebra crossing. Quite dangerous!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crouch Hill, Finsbury Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water leak, 26th June
item.description:
Water leaking out of the road down the road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10, South Vale, Upper Norwood, Greater London

item.description:

item.title: Building materials, 26th June
item.description:
Building materials on pavement and road bricks and sand You bHave to walk in the road to pass this property 14 streamways belvedere
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Streamway, Upper Belvedere, Greater London

item.title: Please dismentle disabled bay, 26th June
item.description:
Disabled bay needs dismentle as owner moved property 36 or 34 cannon road. The bay is no longer needed. tThere is a second disabled bay on the street.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woodlands Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown Tree, 26th June
item.description:
Dangerously overgrown tree limits the path for prople with mobility issues, buggies and wheel chair users. In addition to feeling unsafe at night as it blocks the path.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carstairs Road, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over grown trees, 26th June
item.description:
The trees in the road are overgrown and hanging off ver cars etc.. they need trimming
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kidbrooke Gardens, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree, 26th June
item.description:
Dangerously overgrown tree limits the path for those with mobility issues, buggies and wheel chair users. In addition to feeling unsafe at night as it blocks the path.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carstairs Road, Southend, Greater London

item.title: Flytipped rubbish, 26th June
item.description:
Someone has piled up a huge amount of large cardboard here. It's been through several bin days and hasn't been taken.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Semley Gate, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree, 26th June
item.description:

item.title: Furniture left by the bins, 26th June
item.description:
This has been here over a week

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shrubland Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Raised Ridge, 26th June
item.description:
Raised ridge in the outside line of M25, J27 travelling anti-clockwise. Very dangerous, can easily cause wheel/tyre damage, when hit at 70 mph. Please attend asap.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): M25, Epping Forest
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The road is sinking, 26th June
item.description:
I reported this issue at some stage last year but nothing was done about it and since the heat it has got significantly worse and as it’s at the back of my property I’m very concerned this is going to become serious

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lights in Russia Dock Woodlands not working, 26th June
item.description:
I would like to report that all of the street lights along the walking route from Canada Water Station to Redwood Park, through Russia Dock Woodland, have not been working for several weeks. As a result, the entire route becomes extremely dark after sunset and feels very unsafe - many local residents are worried about walking home in the evening. This is a well-used pedestrian route, and the lack of lighting is causing significant concern for people's safety. Could this please be investigated and repaired as a matter of urgency? Many thanks in advance
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lady Dock Walk, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Orchard - Needs Watering, 26th June
item.description:
The plants in the orchard are just about hanging on. A few are missing and what is left needs watering.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Panfield Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light in front of 41 the Avenue, 26th June
item.description:
The light has not been working since last night (25th June 2025)

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): The Avenue, London
